raw.title: Masonry Top-of-Wall Anchor — ASD | TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3
raw.body_text: Project 💾 Save📂 Load 🖨 Summary📄 Full Calc⊞ Expand All⊝ Collapse Masonry Top-of-Wall Anchor — Fastener Anchorage ASD Headed Anchor Bolt in Masonry · Tension & Perpendicular Shear · TMS 402-22 TMS 402-22 §6.3 & §8.1.4.3 §6.3.2 Projected Breakout Area Apt §6.3.6 Min Embedment §8.1.4.3.1 Tension · §8.1.4.3.2 Shear §8.1.4.3.3 Combined Interaction IBC 1605.2 — ⅓ Allowable Increase Print / Export PDF Save Inputs (.json) Load Inputs (.json) Anderson Rohr Engineering — TMS 402-22 ASD ba (tension) bv (shear) Lb = 4.66" b = 7.625" lbe = 3.81" Near face Far face Ledger / Anchor Plate Section — Anchor in Wall L = 3.81" S = 16.0" b = 7.625" Near face Far face Plan — Projected Breakout Area Apt ■ 1. Material Properties f′m — Masonry Strength (ksi) 1.5 ksi 2.0 ksi 2.5 ksi 3.0 ksi 3.5 ksi fy — Fastener Yield Stress (ksi) 36 ksi — F1554 Gr.36 / A36 55 ksi — F1554 Gr.55 60 ksi — Grade 60 Rebar 105 ksi — F1554 Gr.105 Fu — Fastener Tensile Strength (ksi) Typical: 58 (F1554 Gr.36) · 75 (Gr.55) · 125 (Gr.105). Capped at min(1.9fy, 125 ksi) internally. Fu,eff — Capped Value (ksi, auto) = min(Fu,input, 1.9fy, 125 ksi) ■ 2. Service Loads (ASD — per unit length of wall) Enter service loads (D, L, or D+L). For wind-governed loads, enter the ASD service-level values and use the ⅓ allowable stress increase checkbox below. Do NOT reduce wind loads by 0.6 here. ba — Tension Load (kips/ft) Service tension per linear foot of wall (perpendicular to wall face, e.g. out-of-plane uplift or seismic) bv — Shear Load (kips/ft) Service shear perpendicular to bolt axis (parallel to wall face, e.g. gravity reaction or in-plane shear) ■ 3. Geometry & Fastener Details TMS 402-22 §6.3 b — Wall Thickness (in) 6″ CMU — b = 5.625″ 8″ CMU — b = 7.625″ 10″ CMU — b = 9.625″ 12″ CMU — b = 11.625″ ϕ — Fastener Diameter (in) 1/2″ bolt — Ab = 0.196 in² 5/8″ bolt — Ab = 0.307 in² 3/4″ bolt — Ab = 0.442 in² 7/8″ bolt — Ab = 0.601 in² 1″ bolt — Ab = 0.785 in² 1-1/4″ bolt — Ab = 1.227 in² Lb — Effective Embedment (in) Measured from the masonry surface to the bearing surface of the anchor head or nut (§6.3.1) S — Fastener Spacing (in) Center-to-center spacing along wall length lbe — Edge Distance (in, auto) = b / 2 — measured from edge of masonry to center of bolt (TMS §6.3.7), bolt centered in wall. Override: enter below. lbe Override (in) If blank, auto = b/2 (centered bolt). Enter the edge-to-bolt-center distance if the bolt is not centered. Apply ⅓ allowable stress increase (IBC 1605.2 / TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.1) — use k = 3/4 on demand side, raises combined limit to 1.33. Apply when loads are wind- or seismic-governed. ▶ Run All Checks ✓ ANCHORAGE DESIGN ADEQUATE Max D/C = 0.429 · f′m = 2.0 ksi · ϕ = 0.50″ · Lb = 4.66″ · S = 16.0″ · Ba = 3.468 kips Bv = 1.275 kips ▮ CHECK A — Minimum Embedment · TMS 402-22 §6.3.6 ✓ PASS — Minimum Embedment Check · Max D/C = 0.429 ⇄ ResultsAnchor Allow. Tension Ba: 3.47 kipsAnchor Allow. Shear Bv: 1.27 kipsAnchor T+V Interaction: 0.25 0.50″ Fastener Diameter ϕ 4.66″ Embedment Lb provided 2.00″ Lb,min = max(4ϕ, 2″) required Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Min Embedment Lb ϕ = 0.50″ TMS 402-22 §6.3.6 Lb = 4.66″ Lb,min = 2.00″ 0.429 PASS ▶ Calc ▮ CHECK B — Tension Capacity · TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 ✓ PASS — Tension Capacity (governing: Ba = 3.468 kips) · Max D/C = 0.419 1.453 kips Tension per Fastener S×bₐ (unfactored) 1.453 kips Factored Demand k×S×bₐ 3.468 kips Bₐ Masonry 1.25A₝t√f'm 5.890 kips Bₐ Steel 0.5AᵇFᵤ 3.468 kips Bₐ Governing min(masonry, steel) Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Projected Areas Apt, Apv lb=4.66″, lbe=3.81″, S=16.0″ TMS 402-22 §6.3.2/§6.3.3 Apt = 62.05 in² Apv = 22.80 in² — Info ▶ Calc Tension — Masonry Breakout 1.25Apt√f'm; Apt=62.05 in² TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 k⋅S⋅ba = 1.453 kips Ba,mas = 3.468 kips 0.419 PASS ▶ Calc Tension — Steel Tensile 0.5AbFu; Ab=0.196 in² TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.1 k⋅S⋅ba = 1.453 kips Ba,steel = 5.890 kips 0.247 PASS ▶ Calc ▮ CHECK C — Shear Capacity · TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.2 ✓ PASS — Shear Capacity (governing: Bv = 1.275 kips) · Max D/C = 0.094 0.120 kips Shear per Fastener S×bᵥ (unfactored) 0.120 kips Factored Demand k×S×bᵥ 1.275 kips Bv1 Masonry Breakout 2.582 kips Bv2 Crushing 580(Abf'm)¹ᐟ⁴ 6.937 kips Bv3 Pryout 2.0Bₐᵇ 2.945 kips Bv4 Steel Shear 1.275 kips Bv Governing min(Bv1..Bv4) Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Shear — Masonry Breakout Bv1 1.25Apv√f'm; Apv=22.80 in² TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.2 k⋅S⋅bv = 0.120 kips Bv1 = 1.275 kips 0.094 PASS ▶ Calc Shear — Masonry Crushing Bv2 580(Aᵇf'm)¹ᐟ⁴; Ab=0.196 in² TMS 402-22 Eq 8-5 k⋅S⋅bv = 0.120 kips Bv2 = 2.582 kips 0.046 PASS ▶ Calc Shear — Pryout Bv3 2.5Apt√f'm; Apt=62.05 in² TMS 402-22 Eq 8-6 k⋅S⋅bv = 0.120 kips Bv3 = 6.937 kips 0.017 PASS ▶ Calc Shear — Steel Shear Bv4 0.25AbFu; Ab=0.196 in² TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.2 k⋅S⋅bv = 0.120 kips Bv4 = 2.945 kips 0.041 PASS ▶ Calc ▮ CHECK D — Combined Tension + Shear · TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3.3 ✓ PASS — Combined Tension + Shear · Max D/C = 0.254 1.453 kips Tension/Fastener S×bₐ 0.120 kips Shear/Fastener S×bᵥ 3.468 kips Bₐ Governing (tension capacity) 1.275 kips Bv Governing (shear capacity) 0.254 Interaction Sum (k applied, k=1) 1.00 Limit Eq 8-8 0.254 D/C Combined (= sum / limit) Check Reference Demand Capacity D/C Status Detail Combined T+V Interaction Ba=3.468 kips ; Bv=1.275 kips TMS 402-22 Eq 8-8 (kSba/Ba)5/3+(kSbv/Bv)5/3 = 0.254 ≤ 1.00 0.254 PASS ▶ Calc Engineering Notes: Design per TMS 402-22 ASD §8.1.4.3. Applies to headed anchor bolts or bent bar anchors (standard hook) fully embedded in grouted masonry. lbe (edge distance): Per TMS §6.3.7, measured from the edge of the masonry to the center of the bolt — auto-computed as b/2 for a bolt centered in the wall thickness. Override with the manual field if the bolt is positioned differently. Apt: πlb² per Eq 6-5, less the projected area lying outside the masonry (beyond each wall face) and less overlap with adjacent anchors at spacing S (no portion of masonry counted more than once). Apv: πlbe²/2 per Eq 6-6 (half-cone toward the free edge), with the same overlap adjustment. (TMS §6.3.2, §6.3.3) Tension (Ba): Lesser of masonry breakout (1.25Apt√f′m) and steel tensile (0.5AbFu). Fu capped at min(1.9fy, 125 ksi). Shear (Bv): Minimum of four failure modes: masonry breakout (Bv1), pryout/crushing (Bv2 = 580(Abf′m)0.25), masonry shear cone (Bv3), and steel shear (Bv4 = 0.25AbFu). Combined Check (Eq 8-8): (k·S·ba/Ba)5/3 + (k·S·bv/Bv)5/3 ≤ 1.0. The ⅓ allowable stress increase, when checked, is applied as k = 3/4 on the demands — consistently in the individual checks and the interaction. No ⅓ stress increase: k = 1.0. Minimum embedment (TMS §6.3.6): Lb,min = greater of 4ϕ and 2″. For 3/4″ bolts: Lb,min = 3″. ⇄ Results Hub ⇄ Results Hub× Anchor T+V Interaction0.254 Masonry Top-of-Wall Anchor — ASD | TMS 402-22 §8.1.4.3 · 7/11/2026 Copy Anchor Allow.
